title: Alternative and nuclear energy (% of total energy use)
definition: Clean energy is noncarbohydrate energy that does not produce carbon dioxide when generated. It includes hydropower and nuclear, geothermal, and solar power, among others. This is the share of total energy supply that is non-fossil.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: IEA Energy Statistics Data Browser, International Energy Agency (IEA), uri: https://www.iea.org/data-and-statistics/data-tools/energy-statistics-data-browser, publisher: International Energy Agency (IEA), date accessed: 2025-03-25

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Iceland, Paraguay, Sweden, while the lightest include Libya, Turkmenistan, Trinidad and Tobago; that contrast is the fastest way to read the alternative and nuclear energy pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Alternative and nuclear energy highlights nuclear, hydro, geothermal, and other non-fossil energy systems outside electricity-only shares.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Fossil electricity share can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Women, Business and the Law index overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
Renewable electricity lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Energy maps often separate access, consumption, and production mix.
Look for hydro, fossil-fuel, and low-access signals.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 promotion: interesting non-fossil energy-use signal with nuclear, hydro, and geothermal clues.
Practice-only because it overlaps partly with existing nuclear and hydro electricity shares.
